message:
fix(connectors/grafana): enforce base_url policy at configure (br-1ju3i)

P1 — connectors/grafana previously accepted any base_url verbatim. A
config with `https://attacker:pw@grafana.evil.example/api` parsed,
configured the GrafanaClient with the operator's auth_token bearer
pointing at the attacker host, and started leaking the bearer on every
authenticated request. provisioning_readiness reported a substring-only
"network_ok" check that was purely informational.

Fix: validate_base_url at construction boundary in GrafanaConfig::from_params:
- reqwest::Url::parse + reject userinfo/query/fragment
- require https scheme (or http for localhost / 127.0.0.1 test allowance)
- restrict host to grafana.com or *.grafana.net suffix (Grafana Cloud orgs)

Same shape as br-898o4 / br-ieiby / br-gs71m for zapier / datadog / intercom.

Regression tests cover: default URL accepted, *.grafana.net subdomains
accepted, named-exploit userinfo path rejected, off-policy host rejected,
substring-collision attempts (grafana.net.evil.example, evil.grafana.net.x,
bare grafana.net) rejected, query/fragment rejected, http-non-local
rejected, localhost/127.0.0.1 accepted for tests, garbage URLs rejected.

Operator action: configs pointing at non-policy hosts will now refuse at
configure time. Self-hosted Grafana operators must add their host to the
allow-list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
